name: European Defence Agency
overview: The European Defence Agency (EDA) is the EU's intergovernmental body that helps member states pool defence research and capability development. On AI it acts as a coordinator and convenor rather than a buyer: it runs an Action Plan on Artificial Intelligence for Defence, publishes studies such as the 2025 'Trustworthiness for AI in Defence' white paper, and channels AI-heavy work through its Hub for EU Defence Innovation (HEDI) and a parallel Action Plan on Autonomous Systems. Actual AI procurement and the big money sit with the European Commission's separate European Defence Fund (EDF), with which EDA coordinates.

established.claim: Action Plan on Artificial Intelligence for Defence
established.label: EDA's roadmap for AI cooperation
established.plain: Around 2020-2021 EDA developed an Action Plan on Artificial Intelligence for Defence, presented to its Steering Board alongside a Strategic Research Agenda. The aim is to identify concrete ways for member states to cooperate on military AI, set technology-building-block roadmaps, and steer EDA's AI projects. Most of EDA's later AI studies, including the 2025 trustworthiness white paper, are run under this plan.
established.sourceName: EDA — EDA pursues work on Artificial Intelligence in defence

established.label: Bringing experts together on military AI
established.plain: EDA has run recurring workshops on Defence Applications of Artificial Intelligence to gather industry, academia and ministries of defence. The 2020 edition framed a 'joint quest' for future AI defence applications; the 2021 DAAI workshop fed directly into shaping the AI action plan and technology roadmaps. These events are how EDA builds the shared expert view behind its later publications.
established.sourceName: EDA — Artificial Intelligence: Joint quest for future defence applications

established.label: EDA's innovation arm where AI lives
established.plain: HEDI was set up inside EDA in 2022, mandated by the EU's Strategic Compass, to stimulate and coordinate defence innovation among member states. It prioritises disruptive areas including artificial intelligence and autonomous systems, and runs the operational experimentation campaigns that test AI-enabled drones and robots. It is the practical channel through which much of EDA's AI-adjacent work flows.
established.sourceName: Defence Industry Europe — EDA conducts first EU-level OPEX campaign

established.label: Sister plan covering AI-driven autonomy
established.plain: Published on 16 October 2024, the Action Plan on Autonomous Systems coordinates how the EU develops uncrewed platforms and robots across programmes. AI is named as a key enabling technology alongside robotics, networked systems and advanced computing. The plan covers the full path from research and experimentation through testing, certification, standardisation and ethics.

inProgress.claim: 'Trustworthiness for AI in Defence' (TAID) white paper
inProgress.label: Principles for responsible military AI
inProgress.plain: On 9 May 2025 EDA published a white paper, 'Trustworthiness for AI in Defence', developed under its AI action plan with experts from industry, academia and ministries of defence. It proposes principles of military AI trustworthiness — lawfulness, explicability, robustness and controllability — and reviews the verification, validation and certification needed for trusted defence AI. It is a 'food for thought' document rather than binding regulation.
inProgress.sourceName: EDA — Trustworthiness for AI in Defence white paper (PDF)

inProgress.label: Live tests of AI-enabled drones and robots
inProgress.plain: Under HEDI, EDA ran its first EU-wide Operational Experimentation (OPEX) campaign in summer 2025, hosted by the Italian Army near Rome, focused on autonomous systems for cross-domain (air and land) logistics. Six European firms put AI-enabled unmanned aerial and ground vehicles through realistic tactical scenarios so member states, industry and end-users could jointly evaluate them. OPEX is planned as a recurring, multi-annual effort.
inProgress.sourceName: GlobalSecurity / EDA — First EU-level OPEX campaign tests UAV and UGV systems in Italy

inProgress.label: Standing forum to speed autonomy and AI
inProgress.plain: ASCI is an EDA cooperation platform that links research, testing and deployment to shorten the path from prototype to battlefield for autonomous and AI-enabled systems. Its second conference ran on 4-5 November 2025 in Tallinn, Estonia, gathering some 40 speakers from member states, Ukraine and industry. It exists to share experience and align member states on autonomy and the AI that drives it.

latest.claim: EU Annual Report 2025 highlights defence AI work
latest.label: Year in review names AI as a priority
latest.plain: On 15 April 2026 EDA published its Annual Report 2025, summarising a year in which AI and autonomous systems featured among its innovation priorities, including the OPEX campaign and HEDI-led work. The report frames EDA as delivering value to member states in a demanding security environment. It is the agency's own public account of where its AI-related activity stood at the end of 2025.

latest.claim: Commission's separate EDF round funds 57 projects (incl. AI)
latest.label: Where the EU's defence-AI money actually flows
latest.plain: On 15 April 2026 the European Commission — not EDA — announced 1.07 billion euros for 57 new European Defence Fund projects, with AI named among the critical sectors covered alongside cyber, drones and counter-drone systems. One named example, Project STRATUS, will build an AI-powered cyber defence system for drone swarms. This shows the division of labour: EDA coordinates and studies AI, the Commission's EDF funds the development.
latest.sourceName: European Commission — Commission to invest €1.07 billion in 57 defence projects

suppliers.name: BEYOND VISION (Portugal)
suppliers.claim: BEYOND VISION lists participation in EDA's HEDI/OPEX campaign on its site, presenting its autonomous UAV and ground-control technology for the cross-domain logistics experimentation.

suppliers.name: European Commission (DG DEFIS)
suppliers.role: Funding partner — runs the European Defence Fund that finances AI-heavy defence R&D EDA helps coordinate
suppliers.sourceName: European Commission — European Defence Fund (EDF) official webpage

sources.name: EDA — Trustworthiness for AI in Defence white paper (PDF)
EDA — EDA pursues work on Artificial Intelligence in defence (2021)
European Commission — €1.07bn for 57 EDF defence projects (Apr 2026)
European Commission — AI in Defence factsheet (Dec 2025, PDF)

unknowns: EDA coordinates and studies defence AI rather than buying it, so naming individual AI 'projects' tied directly to EDA (versus the Commission's EDF) is hard from public sources, and detailed contents and funding of EDA's AI Action Plan are not fully public. The exact adoption date of the AI Action Plan is described loosely in public material (work was under way through 2020-2021), and most EDF AI project specifics beyond examples like STRATUS are not individually disclosed.
